sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Kenneth, presented with a 5-day history of right knee pain following a skiing accident where he twisted his knee upon landing.
He reports no hearing of a pop or feeling of anything popping, but describes a sensation of stretching and subsequent swelling.
He has been taking over-the-counter Aleve twice daily and applying a cold pack, which has provided some relief.
He denies any numbness or tingling in his foot.
He reports being able to get out of the water after the incident but experienced pain with squatting or bending.
He has no history of similar knee injuries.
He is a frequent water skier and enjoys outdoor activities.

Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension for which he is currently taking lisinopril 20mg daily, and he reports managing his blood pressure well, with readings of 125/80 mmHg at home.
He had his tonsils removed in the past.
He denies any surgeries or medical conditions.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Lisinopril 20mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/77 mmHg.
Physical exam: On physical examination, the patient has ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion on the right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, a negative varus and valgus test, and a negative Lachman sign.
The dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable, and there is no lower extremity edema.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension; [Condition] [Status: active/suspected/confirmed/past/unknown] [NOT MENTIONED]
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg once daily for pain and inflammation.
I ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee.
I will continue the patient on lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension.
I ordered a lipid panel to check for any potential effects of the hypertension medication.
I administered a tetanus shot for health maintenance.
Follow-up: I will follow up with the patient after the x-ray results are available and to assess the patient's progress with physical therapy.
